pi_items.source: Ford official specifications, BobIsTheOilGuy, Ford Service Content
Ford official specifications, multiple retailers (AMSOIL, BobIsTheOilGuy)
Ford official owner manual and specifications, multiple sources confirmed
RockAuto listings, O'Reilly Auto Parts, Summit Racing. WL10255 Amazon listing explicitly states compatibility with GM/Chevrolet/Cadillac, not Ford.
WP10129 is for 2014-2019 GM vehicles per official listing (not Ford). 24483 is for RAV4 (2006-2018). WP10266 found as correct option for F-150.
Toyota official specifications, AMSOIL, multiple sources
Multiple Toyota sources, Beechmont Toyota, AMSOIL, BobIsTheOilGuy forums
2020 RAV4 Hybrid owner manual states 4.8 quarts. Multiple owner forums note that 4.5 quarts is more accurate in practice.
WebSearch found no results for WL10387 for RAV4. WL10332 confirmed compatible with 2020 RAV4 Hybrid on multiple retailers. WIX 57047 listed as 2006-2018 RAV4, not 2020.
PartsGeek lists WP10322 as 2019-2025 RAV4 cabin filter. 24483 is for 2006-2012/2014-2018 RAV4. WP10168 not found for RAV4.
Honda official specifications, AMSOIL, multiple sources
Honda official specifications, AMSOIL, multiple sources
Honda official specifications, AMSOIL, forum confirmation
RockAuto, O'Reilly, Walmart, Amazon all confirm 57356 for 2018 Civic 1.5T. 51356 exists as WIX part but not specifically verified for 2018 Civic 1.5T.
PartsGeek, Walmart, Amazon confirm 24201 for 2016-2018 Civic. 24815 and WP10084 could not be verified for this model.

privileged_info_summary: The 2019 Ford F-150 5.0L V8 requires 8.8 quarts of 5W-20 oil. The 2020 Toyota RAV4 Hybrid 2.5L requires 4.8 quarts of 0W-16 oil (though 4.5 quarts is practical). The 2018 Honda Civic 1.5L Turbo requires 3.7 quarts of 0W-20 oil. Ford F-150 WIX oil filter options include 57060/57060XP (WL10255 is for GM vehicles only). RAV4 Hybrid WIX oil filters should be WL10332 or 58394DR for 2020 models (not the 2006-2018 era filters like 57047). Honda Civic uses WIX 57356/57356XP for oil. Cabin air filters: Ford F-150 uses WP10266, RAV4 uses WP10322 for 2019-2025 models, and Civic uses 24201. Several claimed part numbers in the metadata are either incorrect (WL10255 for Ford, WL10387 for RAV4) or outdated (57047 for 2020 RAV4, 24483 for 2020 RAV4).
